div.head: The Minister in Nicaragua (Jefferson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Department’s December 11, 3 p.m. The President informs me that he has made an investigation and finds that Nicaraguans are not cutting timber outside of zone agreed upon in my telegram of August 23, 11 a.m., 1918 and Department’s August 28, 6 p.m., 1918.Foreign Relations, 1918, pp. 33–34.
I am of the opinion that it is the President’s intention to carry out agreements made with the Department.
Nicaraguan Minister for Foreign Affairs informs me that in view of the aggressions of the Honduranean Government, he felt it his duty to protest both to the Department and Honduranean Government.
